title: Deduplicate and normalize records
completion_summary: Three new packages: internal/normalizer flattens NULL strings, trims whitespace, and computes a hash over (role || NUL || content || NUL || tool_calls) — including tool_calls in the hash is essential because tool-using sessions emit multiple assistant turns with empty content but distinct tool_calls (the original content-only hash dropped foundation-06's ids 9 and 11 as false dupes; the new hash keeps them). internal/dedup is a thread-safe rolling Window with per-entry expiry and lazy sweeps; injectable clock for tests. internal/state is a tempfile+rename-atomic high-water-mark counter written to <observer_db_dir>/observer.state.json so restarts resume from the last fully-processed messages.id. cmd/serve wires: state.Load → poller.Config.StartFromID = HWM.Get() (unless --from-beginning) → handler(normalize → window.Seen → HWM.Set). Acceptance criteria proven live in the VM: (1) restart idempotency — after run 1 emitted 13 messages and persisted HWM=13, run 2 (no --from-beginning) emitted 0 messages; (2) 60s dedup — unit tests TestWindow_DuplicateInsideWindowIsSeen / TestWindow_DuplicateAfterWindowIsFresh / TestWindow_DifferentSessionsDoNotCollide / TestWindow_SweepEvictsExpired all pass; cross-restart dedup is intentionally HWM's job, not the window's. Deliverables: observer/internal/{normalizer/normalizer.go, dedup/window.go, state/hwm.go} + unit tests + --dedup-window flag.
acceptance_criteria: Same content + same session within 60s = skip
Idempotent on restart — no duplicate records after restart
